question_title: Will Drift Protocol resume perpetuals trading before May 31, 2026?
summary: Drift Protocol's perpetuals trading has been offline since April 1, 2026 following a $295M exploit. With only 6 days remaining before the May 31 deadline, the protocol remains completely offline (DeFiLlama shows $0 volume), the last official update was 19 days ago, and no imminent relaunch has been announced. While the team indicated a May-June 2026 window and has strong Tether backing, the absence of any pre-launch communications, combined with complex prerequisites (new contracts, post-hack audits, USDT migration, new multisig) makes a relaunch within 6 days very unlikely. I estimate 7% probability, reflecting the small but non-zero chance that work has progressed quietly behind the scenes and a surprise launch could materialize.

factors.description: Protocol is completely offline with $0 perp volume confirmed by DeFiLlama as of May 24 - no sign of imminent resumption
Only 6 days remain in the resolution window (May 24-30) - very tight timeframe for deploying new contracts and launching
No specific relaunch date announced - only vague 'May-June 2026' window from product lead
Last official Drift update was May 5 (19 days ago) - absence of pre-launch communications strongly suggests not imminent
Multiple complex prerequisites remain: entirely new smart contracts at fresh address, post-hack audits of new code, USDT migration, new timelocked multisig, potential DAO governance vote
May is within the stated relaunch window, and strong Tether financial backing ($150M) creates urgency to relaunch for revenue generation
One source suggests security reviews covering core protocol are 'completed and published' - possibly clearing an audit blocker
Historical precedent: DeFi protocol relaunches after major exploits typically take months (Euler ~6 months after $197M hack), though Drift has stronger financial backing

reasoning:
## Forecast: Will Drift Protocol resume perpetuals trading before May 31, 2026?

**Probability: 7% (NO is strongly favored)**

### Key Reasoning

**Status quo → NO:** The protocol is completely offline with $0 perp volume as of May 24, confirmed by DeFiLlama. Only 6 days remain in the resolution window.

**Against YES (dominant):**
- No imminent launch announcement — the last official update was May 5 (19 days ago)
- Multiple complex prerequisites remain: new smart contracts at a fresh address, post-hack security audits of new code, USDT migration, new timelocked multisig, potential DAO vote
- Historical precedent: DeFi protocol relaunches after major exploits typically take months
- No testnet/staging activity reported publicly

**For YES (minor):**
- May falls within the stated "May-June" relaunch window
- Strong Tether backing ($150M) creates urgency
- One source suggests some audit work may be completed
- Surprise launches without advance notice do happen in crypto

The 7% reflects the small but real possibility that development has progressed quietly and a surprise launch could occur within the narrow window remaining.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Drift Protocol resume perpetuals trading before May 31, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43584)

[Drift's Comeback: Can a $127.5M Tether Lifeline and a May Relaunch Restore Trust After the $285M Hack?](https://www.ainvest.com/news/drift-comeback-127-5m-tether-lifeline-relaunch-restore-trust-285m-hack-2604/)
[Drift Plans to Relaunch Exchange in May or June 2026](https://intellectia.ai/news/crypto/drift-plans-to-relaunch-exchange-in-may-or-june-2026)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves YES if Drift Protocol's perpetuals exchange resumes live trading for general users at any time from May 17, 2026 through 23:59 UTC on May 30, 2026, such that the DeFiLlama dashboard at https://defillama.com/protocol/drift-trade reports a strictly positive "Perp Volume 24h" value for at least one day during that window when checked on May 31, 2026. Equivalently, if Drift's official channels (drift.trade announcements or @DriftProtocol on X) announce that perpetuals trading has gone live and on-chain perpetuals volume on Drift's Solana program is greater than zero before May 31, 2026, the question resolves YES. Otherwise it resolves NO. If DeFiLlama is unavailable or has not refreshed by resolution, the resolver may use Drift's own official announcements or [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) confirming that live perpetuals trading has resumed before May 31, 2026.
fine_print: "Resume perpetuals trading" means the protocol is open for the general public (not solely the team or whitelisted testers) to place new perpetual futures positions on at least one trading pair. A relaunch on a testnet/devnet, a closed beta limited to internal users, or merely re-enabling deposits/withdrawals without enabling new trades does NOT count. A relaunch that is announced for a future date but has not yet gone live before May 31, 2026 resolves NO.

factors.description: Protocol is completely offline with $0 volume as of May 24 - no sign of imminent resumption
Only 6 days remain in the resolution window (May 24-30) - very tight timeframe
No specific relaunch date announced - only vague 'May-June' window
Last official update was May 5 (19 days ago) - absence of recent pre-launch communications
Multiple complex prerequisites remain: new smart contracts, post-hack audits of new code, USDT migration, new multisig, DAO governance vote
May is within the stated relaunch window, and strong Tether financial backing creates urgency to relaunch
One source suggests audits covering core protocol are 'completed and published', possibly clearing one blocker
Crypto protocol relaunches after major hacks typically take months (Euler ~6 months), though Drift has stronger backing

input.anchor: Base rate for DeFi protocol relaunch within 2 months of $295M exploit: very low historically. Euler Finance took ~6 months after $197M hack. The 'May' half of a 'May-June' window gives roughly 25-30% prior for May relaunch, but we're asking about the last 6 days of May specifically (~20% of remaining May days). Combined rough prior: ~5-8%.
input.assessment:
The evidence overwhelmingly points toward NO resolution. The protocol has been offline for nearly 2 months after a devastating $295M exploit. While the team has laid out a credible recovery plan with strong Tether backing, the relaunch requires deploying entirely new smart contracts, completing post-hack security audits, migrating to USDT settlement, implementing a new multisig governance structure, and potentially passing DAO votes.

With only 6 days remaining and zero public signals of imminent launch (no pre-launch announcements, no testnet activity reports, no 'launching this week' communications), the probability of going live before May 31 is very low. The strongest evidence against is the ABSENCE of any imminent launch signaling - if a protocol is 6 days from launch, you'd expect intense public preparation and communication.

The only factors supporting YES are that May falls within the stated window and there's strong financial pressure to relaunch quickly. But even under the most optimistic interpretation (audits secretly completed, code ready), the lack of any public signal is highly informative.
input.calibration_notes: Anchor is ~6% based on: (a) 'May-June' window gives ~50% chance for May vs June, (b) only ~6/14 remaining May days = ~43% of May window, (c) but conditional on no announcement yet significantly reduces probability. My 7% estimate is consistent with the anchor. Checking for hedging: 7% feels appropriate - not so low as to ignore the stated May window, not so high as to overweight vague timeline statements against concrete absence of launch signals.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether post-hack audits of the new smart contracts have been secretly completed (unclear from public sources). 2. Whether the team could announce and launch within days without advance public notice. 3. Whether 'May-June' window was aspirational vs. realistic planning. 4. Whether DAO governance vote is actually required before launch or can happen concurrently.
input.tool_audit: Research tool provided comprehensive data on the first query (exploit details, recovery plan, TVL/price data, timeline). Second research query failed to access web tools. Web search provided good coverage of news articles and timeline statements. DeFiLlama fetch confirmed $0 volume. X/Twitter posts couldn't be read due to JS requirements. The ainvest.com and intellectia.ai articles were useful for latest timeline and audit status information.
input.update_triggers: - Official Drift announcement of specific relaunch date within the window → would jump to 70-80%. - Testnet/staging activity reported → would increase to 20-30%. - Official statement that relaunch is delayed to June or later → would drop to 1-2%.
input.process_reflection: The research tool worked well for the first query, providing comprehensive data. The second query was less useful due to tool access issues. Direct web search filled the gaps. The biggest friction was inability to read X/Twitter posts (JS-only rendering). For a question like this, being able to check the protocol's latest social media activity would be very valuable. The DeFiLlama fetch was crucial for confirming current status.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Verification Summary

**Resolution criteria recovery:** ✅ The agent successfully fetched the Metaculus question page (question 43584) and correctly identified the resolution criteria: perpetuals must resume for general users with positive DeFiLlama "Perp Volume 24h" between May 17-30, 2026.

**Factor-by-factor trace verification:**

1. **Factor 1 ($0 volume):** Verified — DeFiLlama fetch returned "Perp Volume 24h: $0, Fees 24h: $0, Revenue 24h: $0."
2. **Factor 2 (6 days remaining):** Correct per resolution window (May 17-30 UTC) with question published May 24.
3. **Factor 3 (no specific date):** Verified from ainvest.com and intellectia.ai fetches, which both confirm "May or June 2026" with "no specific date."
4. **Factor 4 (last update May 5):** Verified from drift.trade/updates showing "May 5, 2026 - Recovery Plan for Affected Users" as the most recent post, and web searches for more recent updates returned nothing newer.
5. **Factor 5 (complex prerequisites):** All items traced to the research output: new smart contracts, mandatory audits, USDT migration, timelocked multisig, DAO governance votes.
6. **Factor 6 (Tether backing/urgency):** Grounded in multiple sources confirming Tether's ~$150M support package.
7. **Factor 7 (audits "completed and published"):** Traced to ainvest.com fetch. Agent correctly notes in thinking that these likely refer to pre-hack audits, reflected in appropriately low confidence (0.4).
8. **Factor 8 (Euler ~6 months comparison):** Common DeFi knowledge, approximately correct. Wikipedia tool had 66.7% error rate, but this claim doesn't rely solely on Wikipedia.

**Double-counting:** The agent acknowledged mild correlation between Factor 1 (offline status) and Factor 4 (no communications) in its reflection. Not severe enough to distort the forecast.

**Wrong-direction factors:** All logit signs correct. Negative factors → NO, positive factors → YES.

**Assessment consistency:** Narrative correctly reflects the factor balance — overwhelmingly pointing to NO with minor hedging for surprise launch possibility.

**Counterargument quality:** Adequate — constructs a coherent stealth-mode alternative with specific "what would change my mind" thresholds.

**Self-confidence (0.60):** Appropriate for strong-but-not-certain evidence.

**Probability (7%):** Consistent with the 5-8% anchor and well-supported by evidence. My independent estimate would be in the 4-8% range. No meaningful disagreement.

**Tool audit:** Agent correctly notes second research query failed and X/Twitter was unreadable. Tool metrics confirm 2 errors (66.7% on Wikipedia). No fabricated claims about tool performance.

**Worldview consistency:** The worldview research entry for Drift (slug: drift-protocol-relaunch-status-may-2026-perpetuals-8bcff4c6) is consistent with the factors — it confirms trading is suspended, relaunch target is Q2 2026, and mandatory audits remain prerequisites. No contradictions found.

No hallucinated evidence, no meaningful errors, probability is well-calibrated.
